text: Culpable homicide | Whoever causes death by doing an act with intention of causing death, or with intention of causing such bodily injury as is likely to cause death, or with knowledge that act is likely to cause death | death homicide intention bodily injury knowledge likely to cause death

Murder definition | Culpable homicide is murder if act done with intention of causing death, or with intention of causing bodily injury known to be likely to cause death, or with intention to cause bodily injury sufficient in ordinary course to cause death, or with knowledge that act is so imminently dangerous that it must in all probability cause death | murder death intention premeditation imminent danger bodily injury sufficient to cause death

Transfer of malice - Culpable homicide causing death of person other than intended | If person causes death to someone unintentionally while aiming at another, liability is determined by what was known or intended about the actual victim | transferred intent unintended victim wrong person killed misdirected attack

Punishment for murder | Whoever commits murder shall be punished with death or imprisonment for life and shall also be liable to fine | murder punishment death penalty life imprisonment capital punishment

Mob lynching - Murder by group of 5+ on grounds of race, caste, religion | Murder by group of 5 or more persons acting in concert on grounds of race, caste, sex, place of birth, language, personal belief or any other similar ground | mob lynching group murder hate crime caste violence religious violence communal violence lynch mob

Punishment for murder by life-convict | Murder committed by person under sentence of imprisonment for life shall be punished with death or with imprisonment for life which shall mean imprisonment for remainder of natural life | life convict murder prisoner murder murder in custody repeat murderer

Punishment for culpable homicide not amounting to murder | Culpable homicide not amounting to murder shall be punished with imprisonment for life or imprisonment up to 10 years and fine | culpable homicide not murder manslaughter provocation exceeding right of private defence

Causing death by negligence | Whoever causes death of any person by doing any rash or negligent act not amounting to culpable homicide | negligent death rash act accidental death negligence carelessness

Abetment of suicide of child or person of unsound mind | Abetment of suicide of child, person of unsound mind, intoxicated person, or person under duress | abetment of suicide child suicide unsound mind vulnerable victim instigating suicide
Abetment of suicide | Abetment of suicide if suicide is committed | abetment of suicide instigating suicide aiding suicide encouraging suicide

Attempt to murder | Whoever does any act with such intention or knowledge and under such circumstances that if he caused death he would be guilty of murder | attempt to murder attempted killing failed murder intention to kill

Attempt to commit culpable homicide | Whoever does any act with intention or knowledge that if death caused would be guilty of culpable homicide not amounting to murder | attempt culpable homicide attempted killing failed homicide

Rape definition (consent age raised to 18) | Man commits rape if he penetrates vagina, mouth, urethra or anus of woman with penis or any object or makes her do so with another person or animal, without consent or with consent obtained by putting her in fear of death or hurt, or with consent when she is of unsound mind or intoxicated, or with consent when she is under 18 years | rape sexual assault penetration consent sexual violence minor rape age of consent

Punishment for rape | Punishment for rape - rigorous imprisonment not less than 10 years extending to life imprisonment and fine | rape punishment sexual assault penalty imprisonment for rape victim compensation

Rape on woman under 16 years | Rape on woman under 16 years of age - rigorous imprisonment not less than 20 years extending to life imprisonment | child rape minor sexual assault rape of minor child sexual abuse POCSO

Rape on woman under 12 years | Rape on woman under 12 years - rigorous imprisonment not less than 20 years extending to life imprisonment or death | child rape rape of child under 12 heinous child abuse death penalty for child rape POCSO

Causing death or persistent vegetative state of rape victim | Rape causing death or persistent vegetative state of victim | rape murder rape causing death persistent vegetative state fatal rape death during rape

Sexual intercourse by husband during separation | Sexual intercourse by husband with wife during separation without consent | marital rape during separation separated spouse non-consensual intercourse judicial separation

Sexual intercourse by person in authority | Sexual intercourse by superintendent of jail, remand home, hospital, public servant, manager of hospital, or staff using authority | custodial rape abuse of authority power abuse public servant rape institutional abuse

Sexual intercourse by deceitful means or false promise of marriage | Sexual intercourse by employing deceitful means or making false promise of employment or promotion or marriage or inducing belief that man is her husband | rape by deception false promise of marriage deceitful intercourse promise to marry breach of promise

Gang rape | Gang rape by one or more persons constituting group acting in furtherance of common intention on woman | gang rape group rape multiple perpetrators collective sexual violence Nirbhaya case type
Gang rape on woman under 18 years | Gang rape on woman under 18 years of age | gang rape of minor child gang rape multiple perpetrators on minor group sexual assault on child

Punishment for repeat sexual offenders | Person previously convicted of rape or certain sexual offenses commits rape or causes death while committing rape | repeat rapist serial offender habitual sexual offender second rape conviction
Assault with intent to outrage modesty | Assault or use of criminal force to woman with intent to outrage her modesty | molestation eve teasing outraging modesty sexual harassment unwanted touching

Sexual harassment | Sexual harassment involving unwelcome physical contact or advances, demand for sexual favours, showing pornography, making sexually coloured remarks | sexual harassment unwelcome advances workplace harassment sexually coloured remarks demand for sexual favours

Assault with intent to disrobe (gender neutral) | Assault or use of criminal force with intent to disrobe or compel to be naked - gender neutral provision | disrobing stripping forced nudity public humiliation gender neutral offense

Voyeurism (gender neutral) | Watching or capturing image of woman engaged in private act - gender neutral provision | voyeurism peeping tom capturing private images invasion of privacy non-consensual photography
Stalking | Following, contacting, monitoring use of internet or electronic communication, watching or spying on person | stalking following cyber stalking monitoring harassment unwanted attention

Word or act to insult modesty | Uttering word, making sound or gesture, or exhibiting object intending to insult modesty of woman | verbal harassment insulting modesty obscene gesture lewd remarks cat calling
Theft in dwelling house | Theft in building, tent or vessel used as human dwelling or for custody of property | house theft burglary residential theft dwelling house home invasion

Snatching (newly added distinct from theft) | Theft by suddenly or quickly or forcibly seizing or securing or grabbing or taking away moveable property from person or his possession | snatching chain snatching mobile snatching purse snatching grabbing forcible taking
Theft by clerk or servant of property | Theft of property in possession of master by clerk or servant | employee theft servant theft workplace theft breach of trust embezzlement

Robbery | Theft or extortion where offender voluntarily causes or attempts to cause death, hurt or wrongful restraint or fear of instant death, hurt or restraint | robbery armed robbery violent theft extortion with violence threatening theft

Robbery or dacoity with attempt to cause death or grievous hurt | Robbery or dacoity with attempt to cause death or grievous hurt | violent robbery armed dacoity robbery with weapon attempt to kill during robbery
Dacoity with murder | Dacoity where one of persons committing dacoity commits murder in committing dacoity | dacoity with murder fatal robbery killing during dacoity group robbery murder
Dacoity | Robbery by five or more persons conjointly committing or attempting to commit robbery | dacoity gang robbery organized robbery group of robbers five or more

Snatching (newly added distinct from theft) | Theft is snatching if offender suddenly or quickly or forcibly seizes moveable property from person | snatching quick theft forcible taking grabbing seizing property

Cheating | Whoever by deceiving any person fraudulently or dishonestly induces person to deliver property or consent that any person shall retain property | cheating fraud deception dishonest inducement fraudulent delivery

Cheating by personation | Person cheats by personation if he cheats by pretending to be some other person or by knowingly substituting one person for another | impersonation identity fraud pretending to be someone else substitution fraud

Cheating and dishonestly inducing delivery of property | Cheating and dishonestly inducing delivery of property, making or altering valuable security | property fraud inducing delivery valuable security dishonest cheating

Cheating by personation using computer resource | Cheating by personation by using computer resource or communication device | cyber fraud online cheating phishing computer fraud electronic impersonation identity theft online

Forgery | Making false document or false electronic record with intent to cause damage or injury or to support claim or title or to cause any person to part with property | forgery false document fake document forged signature document fraud
Forgery for purpose of cheating | Forgery for purpose of cheating | forgery for cheating fraudulent forgery document fraud for deception

Forgery of valuable security or will | Forgery of valuable security, will or authority to make or transfer valuable security | forged will valuable security forgery financial document fraud testament forgery
Kidnapping from India or lawful guardianship | Kidnapping any person from India or from lawful guardianship | kidnapping abduction taking person guardian consent unlawful taking

Kidnapping child from lawful guardianship (gender neutral) | Kidnapping or abducting child under 18 years from lawful guardianship - gender neutral provision | child kidnapping minor abduction parental kidnapping child stealing gender neutral
Punishment for kidnapping | Punishment for kidnapping from India or from lawful guardianship | kidnapping punishment abduction penalty
Abduction | Abduction by force compelling or by deceitful means inducing person to go from any place | abduction forcible taking deceitful inducement unlawful removal

Kidnapping or maiming child for begging | Kidnapping or obtaining custody of child to employ or use for purposes of begging, or maiming child for begging | child begging maiming child forced begging child exploitation begging racket

Kidnapping or abducting to murder | Kidnapping or abducting person in order that person may be murdered or disposed of to be put in danger of being murdered | kidnapping for murder abduction to kill murder intent human sacrifice

Kidnapping for ransom | Kidnapping or keeping person in detention threatening death or hurt to compel Government or any person to do or abstain from doing act or to pay ransom | kidnapping for ransom hostage taking ransom demand threatening for money extortionate kidnapping

Kidnapping with intent to secretly and wrongfully confine | Kidnapping or abducting person with intent to secretly and wrongfully confine | secret confinement wrongful confinement unlawful detention hidden captivity

Kidnapping to subject to grievous hurt, slavery, etc | Kidnapping or abducting person to subject to grievous hurt, slavery, or unnatural lust, or knowing it is likely person will be so subjected | kidnapping for slavery sexual exploitation forced labor grievous hurt unnatural lust

Trafficking of person | Recruiting, transporting, harboring, transferring or receiving person by threat, force, coercion, abduction, fraud, deception, abuse of power, or inducement for exploitation | human trafficking sex trafficking forced labor exploitation slavery bonded labor

Exploitation of trafficked person | Exploitation of trafficked person for physical exploitation, slavery, servitude, forced removal of organs, forced labor, drug peddling, prostitution | trafficking exploitation forced prostitution organ trafficking bonded labor sex slavery

Dowry death | Woman death caused by burns or bodily injury or occurs otherwise than under normal circumstances within 7 years of marriage where she was subjected to cruelty or harassment by husband or relative in connection with demand for dowry | dowry death bride burning dowry harassment marriage death cruelty for dowry
Husband or relative subjecting woman to cruelty | Husband or relative of husband subjecting woman to cruelty | domestic violence cruelty to wife marital cruelty in-law harassment dowry harassment

Cruelty defined | Cruelty means willful conduct likely to drive woman to commit suicide or cause grave injury or danger to life, limb or health; or harassment for dowry demand | cruelty definition dowry harassment driving to suicide grave injury mental cruelty
Hurt | Whoever causes bodily pain, disease or infirmity to any person | hurt bodily pain injury causing pain assault

Voluntarily causing hurt | Whoever does act with intention of causing hurt to any person or with knowledge that he is likely to cause hurt | voluntary hurt intentional hurt assault with intent knowingly causing pain
Punishment for voluntarily causing hurt | Punishment for voluntarily causing hurt | hurt punishment assault penalty

Grievous hurt (reduced from 20 to 15 days) | Grievous hurt includes emasculation, permanent privation of sight, permanent privation of hearing, privation of any member or joint, destruction of permanent impairing of powers of any member or joint, permanent disfiguration of head or face, fracture or dislocation of bone or tooth, or any hurt endangering life or causing person to be unable to follow ordinary pursuits for 15 days (reduced from 20) | grievous hurt serious injury permanent injury disfigurement fracture loss of member
Voluntarily causing grievous hurt | Whoever voluntarily causes grievous hurt | grievous hurt serious assault intentional serious injury voluntary grievous hurt
Punishment for voluntarily causing grievous hurt | Punishment for voluntarily causing grievous hurt | grievous hurt punishment serious assault penalty

Causing permanent disability or persistent vegetative state | Voluntarily causing grievous hurt resulting in permanent or likely permanent disability or persistent vegetative state | permanent disability persistent vegetative state permanent injury life-altering injury severe disability

Group of 5+ causing grievous hurt on grounds of caste/religion | Voluntarily causing grievous hurt by group of 5 or more persons on grounds of race, caste, sex, place of birth, language, personal belief | hate crime mob violence caste violence communal violence group assault lynching
Voluntarily causing hurt by dangerous weapons | Voluntarily causing hurt by dangerous weapons or means | weapon assault dangerous weapon armed assault hurt by weapon

Voluntarily causing grievous hurt by dangerous weapons | Voluntarily causing grievous hurt by dangerous weapons or means | grievous hurt by weapon armed assault dangerous weapon grievous hurt weapon violence
Causing grievous hurt by acid attack | Voluntarily causing grievous hurt by use of acid | acid attack acid throwing corrosive attack disfigurement by acid chemical attack

Throwing or attempting to throw acid | Throwing or attempting to throw acid or administering acid to person with intention of causing hurt | acid throwing attempt attempting acid attack acid threat corrosive substance
Act endangering life or personal safety | Act so rashly or negligently as to endanger human life or personal safety of others | rash act negligent act endangering life public safety negligence

Extortion | Intentionally putting person in fear of injury to person, reputation or property to dishonestly induce person to deliver property or valuable security | extortion blackmail threatening for money fear of injury dishonest inducement

Extortion by putting in fear of death or grievous hurt | Extortion by putting person in fear of death or grievous hurt to person or another | severe extortion death threats grievous hurt threats serious blackmail

Criminal intimidation | Threatening person with injury to person, reputation or property to cause alarm or to cause person to do act or omit to do act | intimidation threatening criminal threat coercion menacing

Criminal intimidation by anonymous communication | Criminal intimidation by anonymous communication or having taken precaution to conceal name or abode | anonymous threats concealed identity anonymous intimidation hidden threat

Organised crime (NEW - kidnapping, robbery, extortion, land grabbing) | Organised crime including kidnapping, robbery, vehicle theft, extortion, land grabbing, contract killing, economic offences, cyber-crimes, trafficking, committed by individuals acting in concert as member of organised crime syndicate using violence, threat, intimidation, coercion | organised crime syndicate mafia gang racketeering contract killing land grabbing cyber crime ring

Petty organised crime (NEW - gang theft, snatching, cheating) | Petty organised crime including theft, snatching, cheating, unauthorised selling of tickets, betting and gambling, selling of public exam papers, hawala and illicit foreign exchange, for commercial purpose for gain, by member of organised crime syndicate | petty organised crime gang theft organised snatching ticket touting hawala exam paper leaks organised betting

Terrorist act (NEW) | Act with intent to threaten unity, integrity, sovereignty, security or economic security of India or to strike terror in people, involving murder, grievous hurt, kidnapping, destruction of property, disruption of supplies or services, damage to monetary systems, cyber terrorism | terrorism terrorist act terror attack national security anti-national sabotage cyber terrorism

Acts done by several persons in furtherance of common intention | When criminal act is done by several persons in furtherance of common intention of all, each of such persons is liable for that act as if done by him alone | common intention joint liability group crime shared intention joint criminal enterprise

Criminal conspiracy definition | When two or more persons agree to do or cause to be done illegal act or act by illegal means, such agreement is criminal conspiracy | conspiracy criminal agreement joint planning agreement to commit crime plotting

Punishment for criminal conspiracy | Punishment for criminal conspiracy to commit offence punishable with death, life imprisonment or rigorous imprisonment for 2 years or more | conspiracy punishment planning crime agreement to commit serious offence

Abetment of a thing | Abetment of thing by instigating person, engaging in conspiracy, or intentionally aiding by act or illegal omission | abetment instigation aiding encouraging crime facilitating offence

Punishment of abetment if act abetted is committed | Whoever abets offence shall if act abetted is committed be punished with punishment provided for offence | abetment punishment facilitator punishment aiding completed crime
